name: audit
description: The audit lane. Wraps vendor `owen-it/laravel-auditing` to provide compliance-grade, immutable-after-write field-level diff records for every mutating operation across every module. Answers 'who did what, when?' with a regulator/DPO audience. Zero loss tolerance — every audit row survives a rolled-back transaction (deliberate — the audit trail records the ATTEMPT, not just success). 7-year retention default (extends to 10y with `audit_retention_extended` Enterprise entitlement). Adapts the vendor `audits` table by adding `tenant_id` NOT NULL + `application_id` NULLABLE + composite indexes (tenant_id, created_at) + (application_id, tenant_id, created_at) + (auditable_type, auditable_id, created_at) + (user_type, user_id, created_at) + (event, tenant_id, created_at), and backfilling from existing `new_values.tenant_id` jsonb blobs. Ships two aggregates: `Audit` (the vendor's audits table wrapped with our BelongsToTenant trait + a strict read-only surface), and `AuditRetentionPolicy` (per-tenant retention override — Enterprise tenants can extend past the default 7y with an override_reason + override window). Every module in the codebase adds the vendor's `Auditable` trait to its models; the vendor observer captures create/update/delete/restored + custom events automatically. This module does NOT capture events — it OWNS the storage schema adaptation + query surface + retention scheduling + regulator/DSAR export. The consumer contract is: use the vendor `Auditable` trait; this module handles the rest.
planned_consumers: every module in the codebase that ships mutating aggregates — each adds the vendor `Auditable` trait to its Eloquent models; the vendor observer captures create/update/delete/restored + custom events automatically. This module never enumerates them exhaustively — the consumer count is 'every domain module', not a fixed list.

attribute_driven.description: Audit is NOT attribute-driven — capture is exclusively via the vendor `owen-it/laravel-auditing` package's `Auditable` trait on domain models. There is no `AsAuditableEventCapturer` attribute, no `AsAuditProviderDriver`, no custom capturer registry. The vendor's observer is the single capture path. This module OWNS the storage schema adaptation + read surface + retention + export — never the capture pipeline. Modules opt in by composing the vendor's trait onto their Eloquent models; the vendor observer does the rest.

vendor_adaptation.description: This module adapts the vendor `owen-it/laravel-auditing` package's schema + model contract. The vendor ships an `audits` table with bigint auto-increment PK + polymorphic auditable + polymorphic user + JSON old_values + JSON new_values + created_at. Our adaptation adds `tenant_id UUID NOT NULL` + `application_id UUID NULLABLE` + composite indexes without touching the vendor's own columns.

vendor_package.name: owen-it/laravel-auditing
vendor_package.vendor_observer: OwenIt\Auditing\AuditableObserver — attaches to every model composing the Auditable trait, fires on save/delete/restore/custom, writes an `audits` row.

extensions: Add `tenant_id UUID NOT NULL` column (backfilled from `new_values.tenant_id` for existing rows)
Add `application_id UUID NULLABLE` column (NULL = platform-plane audit)
Add composite indexes for tenant + application + entity + actor + event query patterns
Wrap vendor `Audit` model with our own `Stackra\Observability\Audit\Models\Audit` that adds `BelongsToTenant` trait (global scope + auto-fill on save)
Add read-only enforcement middleware on every /audits route (refuses non-GET methods)
Add AuditRetentionPolicy aggregate for per-tenant retention overrides
Add scheduled PurgeExpiredAuditsJob per-tenant honoring the retention policy
Ship `TenantIdBackfiller` service + `audit:backfill-tenant-id` one-shot migration helper command
vendor_adaptation.primary_key_note: The audit table sticks with the vendor's bigint auto-increment PK. No ULID conversion in Wave 6 — converting the entire vendor schema would require forking `owen-it/laravel-auditing`, and existing customer rows would need re-keying. The `aud_` ULID prefix is REGISTERED in `modules/foundation/data/ulid-prefixes.json` (owned by this module) but RESERVED for a future Wave 7+ redesign; today's audit rows carry bigint IDs matching the vendor.

tenant.description: Tenant-facing audit reads (compliance dashboards) + retention policy CRUD (Enterprise) + DSAR/regulator export request. Every route requires the `audit_capture` entitlement (all tiers baseline); export routes additionally require `audit_export` (Medium+); retention policy CRUD requires `audit_retention_extended` (Enterprise); regulator-shaped exports require `audit_regulator_export` (Enterprise).

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant support tooling. Regulator-issued cross-tenant reads + exports. Runs on the platform_admin guard with cross-application authority.
